skill.name: video-downloader
skill.description: Downloads videos from YouTube and other platforms for offline viewing, editing, or archival. Handles various formats and quality options.
skill.summary: Guides AI assistants through downloading online videos, playlists, audio, metadata, and thumbnails.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown text, fenced examples, and ordinary description words. The skill still has medium operational risk because it instructs an assistant to retrieve external media and write downloaded files locally.

medium_findings.title: External Media Download and Local File Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to download videos from online platforms and save files such as videos and thumbnails locally. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it requires user consent, URL verification, and copyright checks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly describes downloading online videos and saving media files locally. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but the network and filesystem effects are clear.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings are fenced Markdown examples for user prompts and sample output. I found no executable shell, Ruby, or command invocation in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are inside Markdown code fences containing natural language examples and sample transcript text. There is no command syntax or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography findings do not correspond to cryptographic code. The referenced lines contain the skill description and a metadata preservation bullet.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain prose, not hash algorithms, encryption APIs, or credential handling. The static match appears to be a keyword collision.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Examples Are Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are YouTube examples used to explain expected prompts. They do not send data to an unexpected endpoint, but they show that the skill depends on external network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible examples for a video-downloader workflow. No credential transfer, hidden domain, or obfuscation is present in the file.

content.value_statement: Online video workflows often need reliable offline access, audio extraction, or saved reference copies. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear instructions for video, playlist, and metadata downloads.
actual_capabilities: Guides downloads from YouTube and other supported video platforms.
Supports requested quality levels such as 480p, 720p, 1080p, and 4K.
Describes audio-only downloads for podcasts, talks, or music.
Supports playlist and batch download workflows.
Preserves useful metadata such as title, description, and thumbnail.
limitations: Requires a compatible downloader tool or environment outside the skill text.
Cannot bypass platform restrictions, paywalls, DRM, or account permissions.
Users must verify copyright, fair use, and platform terms before downloading.
Large files and playlists may need substantial disk space and bandwidth.

use_cases.title: Save Course Videos
use_cases.description: Download permitted tutorials or lectures for study without a stable internet connection.

use_cases.title: Archive Research References
use_cases.description: Keep copies of public talks, webinars, and interviews that support ongoing research.

use_cases.title: Prepare Creator Assets
use_cases.description: Download owned channel videos, thumbnails, or audio for editing and backup workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Download One Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this video for offline viewing: [URL]. Save it to my Downloads folder.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this video at 720p if available. Use a smaller format if the file is too large: [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Extract Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Download only the audio from this video and save it as an MP3: [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Archive a Playlist
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this playlist, preserve titles and thumbnails, and organize the files in a dedicated folder: [URL].

output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the source URL, selects 1080p when available, reports progress, and saves the video with a readable filename.

output_examples.input: Save the audio from this interview as MP3.
output_examples.output: The assistant downloads the source media, extracts audio only, and reports the saved MP3 location.
The assistant processes each playlist item, keeps titles and thumbnails, and summarizes completed and skipped downloads.

best_practices: Download only content you own or have permission to save.
Confirm the destination folder and expected file size before large downloads.
Use lower resolutions or audio-only mode when bandwidth or storage is limited.
anti_patterns: Do not download copyrighted media for redistribution without permission.
Do not use the skill to bypass access controls, paywalls, DRM, or regional limits.
Do not run large playlist downloads without checking disk space first.

faq.question: Does this skill include a downloader program?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow instructions and expects a compatible downloader tool in the environment.

faq.question: Can it download private or restricted videos?
faq.answer: Only when the user has legitimate access and the platform permits downloading.

faq.question: Can it save audio only?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes audio-only workflows for MP3-style use cases.

faq.question: Can it download playlists?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes playlist and batch download requests.

faq.question: Is this safe for copyrighted content?
faq.answer: Users must check copyright, fair use, and platform terms before downloading any media.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses network sources and saves files locally, so users need consent and source checks.
